title: Israeli Soldiers Fire Tear Gas at Palestinian Youth During Hebron Raid
detail: Israeli soldiers fired tear gas at Palestinian youth during a military raid in Hebron (Al-Khalil), West Bank on April 6, 2026. The incident occurred in the divided city, where approximately 500 settlers live among 30,000 Palestinians under Israeli military administration in the H2 sector. The raid is part of ongoing IDF security operations in the West Bank, which have intensified since October 7, 2023. Tear gas deployment against youth during military raids is a recurring pattern documented by human rights organizations including B'Tselem and OCHA.

media.caption: Israeli soldiers fire tear gas at Palestinian youth during raid in Hebron

title: Israeli Army Fires on WHO Vehicle in Southern Gaza, Killing One
detail: Israeli forces opened fire on a World Health Organization vehicle in southern Gaza on April 6, 2026, killing one person according to medics. The vehicle was clearly marked as a humanitarian convoy. The incident drew immediate condemnation from international health and humanitarian organizations, who called it a direct strike on protected humanitarian personnel. The attack adds to a pattern of incidents involving clearly marked aid and medical vehicles during Israeli military operations in Gaza, raising serious concerns under international humanitarian law regarding the protection of medical and humanitarian workers in conflict zones.

media.caption: WHO employee killed, several injured in Israeli attack in Gaza, say medics
